requirements_for_qualification.summary: Must elect Section 179 expensing to immediately deduct cost of qualifying business equipment and property up to annual dollar limits subject to business income limitation and phase-out thresholds
criteria: Property must be tangible personal property purchased and placed in service in tax year
2025 limits: $1,220,000 maximum expense deduction; $3,050,000 total property phase-out threshold (indexed annually)
Business taxable income limitation: Cannot create loss; limited to taxable income from active trade or business
Property must be used more than 50% for business purposes to qualify for Section 179
New or used property qualifies; no original use requirement like bonus depreciation
Qualified real property eligible: roofing, HVAC, fire protection, alarm systems, security systems (up to $1,220,000 combined)
Must elect Section 179 on timely filed tax return including extensions
Carryforward unused Section 179 (due to income limitation) indefinitely to future tax years

section_179_rules.dollar_limit_2025: $1,220,000 maximum deduction; reduced dollar-for-dollar when total property exceeds $3,050,000
section_179_rules.income_limitation: Deduction limited to taxable business income; cannot create or increase loss
section_179_rules.carryforward_provision: Unused Section 179 (due to income limit) carries forward indefinitely
section_179_rules.qualified_property: Tangible personal property, off-the-shelf software, qualified real property improvements
section_179_rules.business_use_test: Property must be used more than 50% for business; recapture if use drops below 50%
section_179_rules.coordination_with_bonus: Section 179 elected first, then bonus depreciation, then regular MACRS

restrictions: Income limitation often restricts deduction for businesses with limited taxable income
Property purchased from related parties does not qualify for Section 179 election
Property converted to personal use or business use drops below 50% triggers recapture as ordinary income
Real property generally excluded except specific qualified improvement property categories
Listed property subject to strict substantiation requirements and business use percentage tracking
S Corporation and partnership Section 179 passes through to owners subject to their individual income limitations
Phase-out begins when total qualifying property exceeds $3,050,000; dollar-for-dollar reduction
State tax conformity varies; many states have different Section 179 limits creating state/federal differences
